column1: # payload.tsv — the files the hub installs, and where they go. One list, four
# readers:
#   deploy/install.sh                     installs onto a running host
#   image/stage-hub/00-hub/00-run.sh      installs into the image's rootfs
#   image/stage-hub/00-hub/01-run-chroot.sh   enables the units, in the chroot
#   .github/workflows/build-image.yml     stages the files, then asserts every
#                                         row landed in the finished .img
# Each reader has real context the others don't (a native build, ${ROOTFS_DIR},
# an offline chroot), so they stay separate scripts — but the src->dest mapping
# was the same list retyped four times, and a new unit only has to be forgotten
# in one of them to ship an image missing it. Add a unit here and every reader
# picks it up.
# Format: whitespace-separated, '#' comment lines and blanks skipped. Readers
# parse it with a process substitution, never a pipe — a pipe runs the loop body
# in a subshell, so anything it accumulates (install.sh collects the units to
# enable) is silently lost at `done`:
#   while read -r src dest mode enable on_host; do
#   done < <(grep -Ev '^[[:space:]]*(#|$)' payload.tsv)
#   src      path relative to pi/
#   dest     absolute path on the hub (image readers prefix ${ROOTFS_DIR})
#   mode     install -m
#   enable   yes = a systemd unit to enable; '-' = a plain file
#   on_host  what deploy/install.sh does with the row. The image ignores this
#            column and bakes every row: no hardware exists at build time, so
#            there is nothing to test against. Values:
#              always  install unconditionally
#              wifi    only on a host that has a Wi-Fi radio
#              image   skip — baked into the Pi image only
# src                                 dest                                             mode  enable  on_host
# The dashboard/HTTP chassis. (The hubd binary itself is not a row: install.sh
# builds it natively, the image takes it from a CI artifact — the one file
# whose source genuinely differs per context.)
deploy/hubd.service                   /etc/systemd/system/hubd.service                 0644  yes     always
# Zenoh: the router (zenohd) + the browser edge (ws-adapter) — the transport.
# The two units and the two plain files (the router config, the adapter script)
# are rows; the zenohd BINARY and the adapter's VENV are NOT — a download and a
# venv, provisioned by install.sh / the image chroot (see below). The operator
# credential is generated, not copied — seeded into /etc/hub/operator.env.
deploy/zenohd.service                 /etc/systemd/system/zenohd.service               0644  yes     always
deploy/ws-adapter.service             /etc/systemd/system/ws-adapter.service           0644  yes     always
zenoh-router.example.json5            /opt/hub/zenoh/zenoh-router.json5                0644  -       always
ws-adapter/ws_zenoh_adapter.py        /opt/hub/ws-adapter/ws_zenoh_adapter.py          0644  -       always
# Day-zero hub-XXXX AP (open, 10.42.0.1) — a hub nobody can find isn't a hub.
# The profile is created once on first boot; NetworkManager owns it after.
# The setup script picks the built-in radio by driver, never by interface name.
deploy/hub-ap-setup.sh                /usr/local/bin/hub-ap-setup.sh                   0755  -       wifi
deploy/hub-ap.service                 /etc/systemd/system/hub-ap.service               0644  yes     wifi
# Uplink radio watchdog — the USB dongle's driver wedges when the venue AP goes
# away, and only a module reload clears it. Gated on "is this a Wi-Fi host at
# all", the same test as the AP above, and NOT on the wedging driver being bound
# right now: the dongle may be unplugged at install time and arrive later, and a
# watchdog that silently wasn't installed is the failure it exists to prevent.
# The script no-ops when its driver is absent.
deploy/hub-uplink-watchdog.sh         /usr/local/bin/hub-uplink-watchdog.sh            0755  -       wifi
deploy/hub-uplink-watchdog.service    /etc/systemd/system/hub-uplink-watchdog.service  0644  yes     wifi
# USB-C recovery plane: ECM ethernet (ssh pi@10.55.0.1) + ACM serial. Image-only
# — it needs the dwc2 peripheral-mode overlay on the boot partition, which
# install.sh (host-agnostic, any systemd box) has no business writing.
deploy/usb-gadget-setup.sh            /usr/local/bin/usb-gadget-setup.sh               0755  -       image
deploy/usb-gadget.service             /etc/systemd/system/usb-gadget.service           0644  yes     image
# Hub IP + router status on every interactive login (serial autologin and ssh
# both source /etc/profile.d/*.sh). Image-only: it answers "what's my hub's
# address" for the appliance's recovery console, which a generic host has not got.
deploy/hub-login-banner.sh            /etc/profile.d/hub-status.sh                     0644  -       image
